findings.title: Use of os.system()
findings.description: os.system() may lead to command injection if arguments include untrusted input.
findings.recommendation: Use subprocess.run() with a list of arguments and avoid shell interpretation.
findings.suggested_fix: Use subprocess.run() with a list of arguments and avoid shell interpretation.
findings.remediation: Use subprocess.run() with a list of arguments and avoid shell interpretation.

findings.title: Use of pickle.loads()
findings.description: pickle deserialization may execute arbitrary code when loading untrusted data.
findings.recommendation: Do not deserialize untrusted pickle data. Use safer formats such as JSON where possible.
findings.suggested_fix: Do not deserialize untrusted pickle data. Use safer formats such as JSON where possible.
findings.remediation: Do not deserialize untrusted pickle data. Use JSON or other safe formats.

findings.title: Use of yaml.load()
findings.description: yaml.load() may be unsafe when parsing untrusted YAML content.

findings.title: Use of eval()
findings.description: eval() executes dynamic Python code and may allow code injection.
findings.recommendation: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing, explicit logic, or restricted parsing libraries.
findings.suggested_fix: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing, explicit logic, or restricted parsing libraries.
findings.remediation: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing, explicit logic, or restricted parsing libraries.

findings.title: Use of os.system()
findings.description: os.system() may lead to command injection if arguments include untrusted input.
findings.recommendation: Use subprocess.run() with a list of arguments and avoid shell interpretation.
findings.suggested_fix: Use subprocess.run() with a list of arguments and avoid shell interpretation.
findings.remediation: Use subprocess.run() with a list of arguments and avoid shell interpretation.

findings.title: Taint flow: user input -> os.system
findings.description: Tainted data from user input flows into os.system, which may allow command injection.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitize user input; use parameterized queries for SQL; avoid shell=True.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate and sanitize user input; use parameterized queries for SQL; avoid shell=True.
findings.explanation: Tainted data from user input flows into os.system, which may allow command injection.
findings.remediation: Use allowlists and avoid shell=True; prefer subprocess with list args.

findings.title: Taint flow: user input -> cursor.execute
findings.code_snippet:
33:     query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name=" + name
>>   34:     cursor.execute(query)
findings.snippet:
33:     query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name=" + name
>>   34:     cursor.execute(query)
findings.description: Tainted data from user input flows into cursor.execute, which may allow sql injection.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitize user input; use parameterized queries for SQL; avoid shell=True.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate and sanitize user input; use parameterized queries for SQL; avoid shell=True.
findings.explanation: Tainted data from user input flows into cursor.execute, which may allow sql injection.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

findings.title: Verify sensitive files are not tracked
findings.type: Verify sensitive files are not tracked
findings.description: .gitignore only prevents untracked files from being added; it does not untrack or remove files already in the index. Sensitive or cache files already committed remain in repository history until explicitly removed.
findings.recommendation: Run 'git ls-files' to list tracked files. For any .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, or venv paths: use 'git rm --cached <file>' to stop tracking. Rotate any exposed secrets.
findings.suggested_fix: Run 'git ls-files' to list tracked files. For any .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, or venv paths: use 'git rm --cached <file>' to stop tracking. Rotate any exposed secrets.
findings.remediation: Run git ls-files and use git rm --cached for any .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, venv.
